mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS level remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts confirms broad-based economic demand growth, driving sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observed quarters, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during an overheating economy indicates supply bottlenecks and capacity constraints, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during overheating regimes does not predict negative 5-day returns in at least 50% of cases, or the regime-conditional signal shows Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 220 trigger inflation expectations and anticipated central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding corporate revenue capacity and consumer spending power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling industrial production signals inventory accumulation and weakening downstream demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-INDPRO divergence signal predicts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated industrial production combined with high inflation levels creates a stagflationary supply shock environment, forcing discount rate hikes and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: High INDPRO and CPIAUCSL co-occurrence fails to predict negative 60-day returns in over 55% of observed quarters, or the joint signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.
